Use a Sliding Window approach. Maintain a `left` pointer and a `right` pointer to represent the current substring.
As you move the `right` pointer, check if the character already exists in your current window.
You can use a HashSet to track characters in the window. If you see a duplicate, keep removing characters from the `left` until the duplicate is gone.
Optimization: Instead of a Set, use a HashMap to store the character and its exact index. This lets you jump the `left` pointer directly past the last seen index instead of stepping one by one.
